title: Masala Makhana
description: Crispy spiced fox nuts (lotus seeds). A light tea-time and travel snack that beats namkeen.
explanation: Makhana is naturally light and becomes addictive when roasted with a little oil and spice. It is a smart swap for fried chips when you want something crunchy with chai or on a train journey.
Roast until they sound hollow and crisp—under-roasted makhana tastes chewy.
ingredients: 1 tbsp Oil or 1 tsp for lighter version
instructions: Dry-roast makhana in a wide pan on medium heat for 6–8 minutes, stirring, until crisp.
Push makhana aside, add oil and spices to the pan, and bloom for a few seconds.
Toss makhana until evenly coated. Adjust salt.
Cool completely before storing in an airtight jar.
stepNotes: Cooling fully is required for lasting crunch.
If spices stick poorly, mist with a few drops of water or lemon before tossing.
Air fryer: 160°C for about 6–8 minutes, shaking once, then toss with spices.
Skip oil and use a non-stick pan for a lighter batch—spices cling a bit less.
Stays crisp 5–7 days in an airtight container.

variationSections.title: Flavor profiles
items: Peri-peri or pizza seasoning for kids and cravings.
Jaggery + cinnamon for a sweet snack.
Curry leaves and mustard seeds for a South Indian tempering feel.

variationSections.title: Snack pairings
items: Mixed with roasted peanuts for more calories.
Packed in a small pouch for flights and long drives.
